summary: Frontend-only validation of MemoryMate hardening pass. All 8 review items passed end-to-end via Playwright on the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL preview. Token refactor verified (mm_token in localStorage, persists across reload, normal login + 3 demo buttons all route correctly). Emergency page renders with Sarah Ahmed contact and both call/message buttons. Private Mode toggle ON shows the dark banner, persists across reload, server-side blocks capture start with HTTP 423 (toast-surfaced), and toggles OFF cleanly. Memory Capture routing: a 2-fact transcript ('Sarah reminded me about the doctor appointment ... 3 PM ... take my blood pressure medicine every morning') produced 2 memory event cards (1 appointment + 1 medication) and, after switching to the caregiver demo account, the new doctor/clinic appointment is visible on /caregiver/appointments and the blood-pressure medication is visible on /caregiver/medication — routing into the existing tables works. Privacy Review EDIT action: review-action-edit reveals review-edit-input + review-edit-save; saving an edited text resolves the item (9 -> 8 pending). Meeting Mode regression still produces a structured meeting-summary block with Key points / Decisions / Action items / Follow-ups. Mobile (390px) shows no horizontal overflow on patient home or caregiver dashboard; tap targets are appropriately large.
critical_code_review_comments: Token logic correctly centralised in src/lib/token.js (getToken/setToken/clearToken/isTokenExpired). isTokenExpired tolerates malformed JWTs (returns true) — good.
api.js request interceptor proactively clears expired tokens; response interceptor clears on 401. Clean and defense-in-depth.
Login.js no longer ships hardcoded demo passwords — demoLogin(role) hits POST /api/auth/demo-login. Security/maintainability win.

Emergency.js never fails silently: 'patient' load error switches to a fallback red-card screen with a retry button (emergency-retry-btn) AND the alert POST has its own catch with a clear toast — both paths visible to the user. The tel: link still works even when the in-app alert fails.

PrivacyReview.js: edit flow toggles a textarea+save inline (review-edit-input / review-edit-save) and calls act(it,'edit', editText). Backend ReviewAction is verified to accept 'edit' (per prev iteration). Cancel button present.

CaptureSession.js routes appointment/medication into the patient_id-scoped existing collections (verified via caregiver UI), confirming backend capture routing is wired through the frontend correctly.
Mobile: Patient home uses a 2-column tile grid that holds at 390px without overflow; toggle row is full-width with large hit area.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@memorymate.app/admin123 | Patient: omar@memorymate.app/Patient123! | Caregiver: sarah@memorymate.app/Caregiver123! | Demo buttons: server-side, no creds.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_CaptureRouting capture session (routed into appointments + medications collections for Omar) and 1 TEST_MeetingRegression meeting session on the caregiver side. 1 privacy-review item was resolved via the edit flow (pending count 9 -> 8). These persist in the DB but are clearly TEST_-prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend public URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env. All flows in this hardening pass verified — no regressions found. AI capture/process takes ~5-15s; use >=60s timeouts for session-summary. Use data-testid for everything. For toggling Private Mode in tests, restore to OFF at end to avoid blocking later capture-session tests. The capture routing UI lives on patient side at /patient/capture (start) and the meeting variant at /caregiver/meeting and /patient/meeting; both share CaptureStart + CaptureSession components.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
